options.why: Multiplied $8$ by $y$; read 'more than' as 'lots of' instead of addition.
Subtracted $y$ from $8$; wrong operation and reversed order.
Wrote the numbers in reading order; 'less than' subtracts from $x$, giving $x - 6$.
Multiplied; read 'less' as a grouping word instead of subtraction.
Bracketed the subtraction; tripling applies to $n$ only, not to $n - 4$.
Read 'triple' as 'cubed' instead of multiply by $3$.
Reversed the subtraction; the $4$ is taken from the result, not the reverse.

questions.question_text: Which expression represents **"$5$ is added to $x$, then the result is doubled"**?

options.why: Doubled only $x$; 'the result' is the whole sum $x + 5$, which needs brackets.
Doubled the $5$ only and left $x$ unchanged.
Added $2$ instead of doubling; 'doubled' means multiply by $2$.
Describes $2(n + 3)$; here the doubling applies to $n$ only, not to $n + 3$.
Uses subtraction; the $+3$ means add $3$, not subtract it.
Swapped the coefficient and constant; here $2$ is the multiplier and $3$ is added.

questions.solution_text:
$2n = \text{double } n$
$2n + 3 = 3 \text{ more than that}$
$3$ more than double $n$

questions.question_text: Which expression represents **"$3$ is subtracted from $k$, then the result is multiplied by $8$"**?

options.why: Multiplied only $k$ by $8$; the result $(k - 3)$ must be bracketed before multiplying.
Reversed the subtraction; 'subtracted from $k$' means $k - 3$, not $3 - k$.
Reversed the order and multiplied only $k$; both the order and the brackets are wrong.
